item.title: Politician Moshiur Rahman Jadu Mia remembered
item.description: Politicians, academics and professionals on Saturday said that politician Moshiur Rahman Jadu Mia, a close associate of Maulana Abdul Hamid Khan Bhashani, wanted to establish a democratic Bangladesh....

item.title: Mirza Abbas to be flown to Singapore for advanced treatment
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party standing committee member and adviser to the prime minister Mirza Abbas is set to be flown to Singapore today for advanced medical treatment following brain surgery in Dhaka....

item.title: Sale frustrates publishers
item.description: The 18-day Amar Ekushey Book Fair will end today. Publishers and salespersons said that although many visitors thronged the fair on holidays, the overall sales were not satisfactory....

item.title: Iranian envoy says Bangladeshi tankers can pass Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Bangladesh has received reassurance from Iran regarding the safe passage of fuel shipments amid growing concerns about global oil supply disruptions....

item.title: BNP leaders made administrators of 5 more city corporations
item.description: The government on Saturday appointed five leaders of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party as administrators at five more city corporations in the country....Maps

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 6 in Gaza
item.description: Gaza’s civil defence agency said Saturday that Israeli strikes had killed six people over the past 24 hours, the latest bout of violence in the territory against the backdrop of the US-Israeli war with Iran....

item.title: Protesters burn Trump, Meloni’s photos in Rome
item.description: Protesters burned images of US president Donald Trump and Italy’s prime minister Giorgia Meloni in Rome Saturday during a demonstration against the government’s judicial reform and the Middle East war....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Joy at death and destruction
item.description: THE United States and Israel are both reveling in inflicting the maximum possible death and suffering on Iran......

item.title: Reforms in recruitment
item.description: A RECENT notification issued by the Secondary and Higher Education Division drew attention through a news report. According to the gazette, 82 office staff — including stenographers-cum-computer operators, accounts clerks, upper-division assistants and head assistants — have been promoted to the post of assistant upazila secondary education officer. These......

item.title: People begin leaving Dhaka ahead of Eid
item.description: People have started leaving the capital by trains, buses and launches for their ancestral homes in different districts to celebrate Eid-ul-Fitr with their near and dear ones....

item.title: Matiur Rahman new NOAB president, Shahidullah Khan vice-president
item.description: The Newspapers Owners’ Association of Bangladesh has elected its new executive committee with Matiur Rahman Chowdhury, chief editor of the daily Manab Zamin, as its president and ASM Shahidullah Khan, chairman of New Age editorial board, as vice-president....News analysis reports

item.title: Informal sector precarity
item.description: BANGLADESH’S development story is often narrated through the lens of export growth, poverty reduction, and steady GDP expansion. However, behind these macroeconomic achievements lies a structural risk: the fragile state of its large informal workforce. The contradiction is clear: as Bangladesh advances toward middle-income status, most of its workers......

item.title: Jamaat, allies threaten street movement if constitution reform council session not convened
item.description: The alliance led by the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Saturday called on the Bangladesh National Party-led government to convene today a session for the Constitutional Reform Council to implement the July National Charter or else, the alliance said, they would......

item.title: From aid recipient to knowledge producer
item.description: ON A humid Dhaka morning, development can sometimes feel like paperwork—projects, logframes, missions and memos. Yet, the most consequential shift now unfolding is quieter. The next chapter may be defined not by how much assistance it receives, but by how much knowledge it generates and shares. This is not a slogan for conferences. It reflects a changing......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Fuel oil dealers threaten strike from tomorrow
item.description: Petroleum dealers and pump owners have threated the government to go on a nationwide strike from tomorrow amid supply shortages of octane and diesel....

item.title: Zero-tolerance against cheating, question leaks in SSC exams: education minister
Tangail weaving artisanry must be protected
item.description: TANGAIL sari, recognised for its century-old tradition of artisan excellence, is struggling to survive in the face of mechanised fabric production and changing fashion choices. In December 2025, the detailed designs and fine textures of the garments made in the central Tangail city won UNESCO recognition as intangible cultural heritage, but the global acclaim has failed to......

item.title: Regular curriculum review vital for education quality
item.description: THIS is a good piece of news that primary, secondary and higher secondary curriculums may get over the uncertainty that they have been faced with in the changedpoliticalcontext since the fall of the Awami League government in August 2024. The education ministry in 2024 reverted to the curriculum of 2012, put into effect in 2013, discontinuing the 2022 curriculum that......

item.title: Bangladesh face Pakistan in series decider today
item.description: Bangladesh want to make a fresh start and secure the three-match ODI series when they take on Pakistan in the third and final game at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium today......

item.title: Dorielton treble fires Kings
item.description: Brazilian forward Dorielton Gomes scored a hat-trick as table toppers Bashundhara Kings beat Fortis Football Club 3-2 in their return-leg tie of the Bangladesh Football League at the Kings Arena in the city on Saturday......

item.title: Gavi ready to play after injury: Flick
item.description: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said young midfielder Gavi is able to face Sevilla on Sunday in La Liga after a long injury lay-off......Maps

item.title: Trump urges world leaders to secure Hormuz
item.description: US president Donald Trump urged other nations to help secure a vital shipping lane choked off by the war with Iran that showed no signs of slowing on Saturday as strikes hit the US embassy in Baghdad and a major Emirati energy facility....Age-specific news

item.title: Carrick shrugs off Scholes dig
item.description: Michael Carrick on Friday brushed off criticism from former Manchester United teammate Paul Scholes as he prepares his side for a crunch Premier League game against Aston Villa......

item.title: Russian strikes kill 4 in Kyiv region
item.description: Russian drone and missile strikes near Ukraine’s capital and several other regions killed four people and wounded 15, the authorities said Saturday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Trump admin lashes out at CNN over Iran war
item.description: The Pentagon and White House slammed CNN over its Iran war coverage Friday—with defence secretary Pete Hegseth saying he looked forward to an ally of president Donald Trump taking over the news network....

item.title: Türkiye to support Bangladesh’s candidacy for UNGA presidency
item.description: Türkiye on Saturday expressed its unequivocal support for Bangladesh’s candidacy for the presidency of the forthcoming session of the United Nations General Assembly at a bilateral meeting in Ankara....

item.title: ME turmoil revives Norway push for Arctic drilling
item.description: Already the continent’s biggest single supplier of natural gas since Russia invaded Ukraine, Norway is hoping to use the Middle East war to get European Union blessing to drill in the Arctic....

item.title: Question paper leakage, unfair means in SSC exams to be strictly monitored: Milon
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Saturday said that the government had taken a zero-tolerance policy to prevent question paper leaks and adoption of unfair means in the upcoming Secondary School Certificate and equivalent examinations....News analysis reports

item.title: Bangladesh envoy visits injured, pays respects to victims
item.description: The Bangladesh high commissioner to the Maldives, Md Nazmul Islam, visited the injured and paid respects to the deceased following a deadly gas cylinder explosion at a guesthouse on Dhigurah Island, about 100 kilometers from Male, on the night of March 12, 2026....

item.title: DMP warns of strict action against unfit buses during Eid travel rush
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police has announced a series of security and traffic management measures, including strict action against unfit buses, to ensure smooth and safe travel from the capital to different districts ahead of Eid-ul-Fitr....

item.title: College student killed in Dhaka road crash
item.description: A college student was killed when a covered van hit his motorcycle at Nagdarpara of Khilgaon in Dhaka on Friday night....

item.title: Mobile phone dispute ends in teen murder in Sherpur
item.description: A 14-year-old boy died in a hospital after being attacked by a local teen gang in Sherpur on Saturday....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Outsider detained for filming student taking shower in DU hall bathroom
item.description: An outsider was detained while allegedly filming a Dhaka University student taking shower at a Bijoy Ekattor Hall bathroom....

item.title: 21 people arrested at Jatrabari
item.description: The police arrested 21 people during a special operation at Jatrabari in Dhaka on Friday night over their alleged involvement in various criminal activities....

item.title: 1,500 litres of petrol and octane seized at cowshed in Pabna
item.description: The Department of Consumer Rights Protection seized 1,500 litres of petrol and octane illegally stored in a cowshed at Chinakhara under Sujanagar in Pabna on Saturday....

item.title: Closed jute mills to be reopened: Shariful Alam
item.description: State minister for textiles and jute Md Shariful Alam has said that the closed jute mills across the country would be reopened to increase employment opportunities....City & Local Guides

item.description: Speakers at a discussion in Dhaka on Saturday stressed that the country’s struggle to safeguard its sovereignty must go on....

item.title: Rickshaw puller found dead in Khulna
item.description: A rickshaw puller, who went missing three days ago, was found dead at Jailkhana Kheya Ghat under Khulna Sadar in the district on Saturday....

item.title: 37 Rohingyas who fled Bhasan Char held in Sandwip
item.description: The Bangladesh Navy and the Bangladesh Police in a joint operation apprehended 37 Rohingyas, who escaped from the Bhasan Char shelter camp, in the Beribandh area of Santoshpur union under Sandwip in Chattogram on Friday night....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Woman hacked to death in Chuadanga
item.description: THE police recovered the body of a woman with her hands and feet tied and bearing multiple injuries, from a tobacco field at Daliapur village of Damurhuda upazila in Chuadanga on Friday night....

item.title: Worker falls off four-storey building, dies in Rajshahi
item.description: A construction worker died after falling from the fourth floor of an under-construction building in Rajshahi city on Saturday morning....

item.title: PADMA BRIDGE RAIL LINK: Narail commuters face long waits as only one train runs on key routes
item.description: Passenger train services on Dhaka-Narail-Khulna and Dhaka-Narail-Benapole routes are currently operating with only one train, forcing travellers to rely on limited schedules and often wait long hours for the next journey....

item.title: Nationwide mosquito control campaign launched
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain launched a nationwide mosquito control and cleanliness campaign to prevent dengue and other mosquito-borne diseases on Saturday....News analysis reports

item.title: Woman’s dismembered body found
item.description: The police on Saturday recovered dismembered body of an unidentified woman from an abandoned pond in the Sreepur Municipality area of ​​Gazipur....

item.title: TK 11.41cr handed over to land victims in Sylhet
item.description: The cheques amounting to TK 11.41 crore were distributed among the owners of land acquired for four development projects under implementation in Sylhet district....

item.description: Daffodil Polytechnic organised Laptop Distribution Ceremony 2026 with the aim of developing skilled and confident diploma engineers through technology education....

item.title: Fire Service distributes awareness leaflets
item.description: Fire Service and Civil Defence distributed leaflets among all visitors and book stalls at Amar Ekushey Book Fair on Saturday to raise awareness on preventing accidents including fire....Bangladesh news updates
